tracking_policy: Internal peers and adopted source concepts use exact commits. External tools that may be installed or cloned must be refreshed against their authoritative source before adoption. A reference never overrides Continuum contracts, repository-owned validators, or application runtime authority.

references.name: Foundry
references.authority: Branch/repository intelligence: Git and provider facts, drift, staleness, capabilities, readiness, storage, and portfolio views.
references.continuum_role: Consume read-only recovery candidates and govern replay, provenance, validation, and closure.
references.adoption_boundary: Do not duplicate Foundry dashboards/storage or delegate workflow authority to its current no-op policy seam.
references.risk: Its default branch is a dated feature branch; no canonical main was resolved. FoundryPolicy is permissive.
references.next_experiment: Define a read-only recovery-candidate packet with exact PR/branch/commit/path/hunk/comment provenance.

references.name: The Blacksmith Guild
references.authority: Application runtime policy, adapters, proof taxonomy, validators, and TbgContinuumCapabilityPacket producer.
references.continuum_role: Import proven generic capability descriptions read-only after extraction gates.
references.adoption_boundary: Gameplay, launcher, save, route, trade, economy, smithing, and runtime authority remain app-owned.
references.risk: Large open/stacked PR inventory; active runtime lanes are not stale-replay inputs.
references.next_experiment: Import TbgContinuumCapabilityPacket.v1 read-only before extracting implementation.

references.name: WezTerm
references.authority: Terminal, multiplexer, pane, tab, window, and domain runtime behavior.
references.continuum_role: Emulate named-domain capability/lifecycle boundaries; use as an optional operator shell.
references.adoption_boundary: Do not vendor terminal runtime code or claim state without independent observation.
references.risk: Provider integration can collapse operator ergonomics, execution, and proof into one unsafe surface.
references.next_experiment: Disposition Continuum PR #5 before a read-only state observer.

references.name: tmux
references.authority: Server, session, window, pane, and client lifecycle.
references.continuum_role: Optional session backend and durable attach/detach prior art.
references.adoption_boundary: Pane/session presence never proves command or application success.
references.risk: Session presence can be mistaken for process health.
references.next_experiment: Define a provider-neutral session snapshot first.

references.name: Neovim
references.authority: Editor, buffer, LSP, and user-configuration behavior.
references.continuum_role: Expose compact files, commands, diagnostics, and task packets.
references.adoption_boundary: Do not vendor personal configuration or treat editor state as proof.
references.risk: Machine-local plugins can become false repo dependencies.
references.next_experiment: Keep integration operator-local until the command surface stabilizes.

references.name: Vercel Labs skills CLI
references.authority: Agent-skill installation, discovery, updates, and distribution conventions.
references.continuum_role: Publish narrow project-local skills that route agents to contracts.
references.adoption_boundary: Skill text is guidance, not execution authority; review sources before use.
references.risk: Broad installs expand prompt and trust surfaces.
references.next_experiment: Package one read-only repo-floor skill and test install/discovery/removal.

references.name: OpenSuperWhisper
references.continuum_role: Accept dictated text only as untrusted operator input.
references.adoption_boundary: Voice input cannot bypass scope, confirmation, provenance, or validation.
references.risk: Primary operator environment is Windows; transcription errors can become unsafe instructions.
references.next_experiment: Keep a provider-neutral voice-input boundary and defer adoption.

references.name: Agent eXperience Interface
references.authority: Agent-oriented CLI principles and compact structured interfaces.
references.continuum_role: Use stable identifiers, bounded output, discovery, and progressive disclosure.
references.adoption_boundary: Token efficiency cannot weaken evidence or human-readable reporting.
references.next_experiment: Audit one command against AXI without behavior changes.

references.name: lavish-axi
references.authority: Local HTML review, annotation, Mermaid editing, and agent polling.
references.continuum_role: Optional visual review for architecture and sprint artifacts.
references.adoption_boundary: Annotations remain untrusted input until captured in a bounded task.
references.risk: Polished review can be mistaken for validation.
references.next_experiment: Review one generated map and preserve accepted annotation provenance.

references.name: no-mistakes
references.authority: Local git proxy and disposable-worktree validation pipeline.
references.continuum_role: Optional delivery gate; emulate fail-closed checks and safe repair.
references.adoption_boundary: Continuum topology and repo validators remain authoritative.
references.risk: A proxy can conflict with repo checks or obscure mutation ownership.
references.next_experiment: Evaluate one docs-only branch and compare evidence models.

references.name: gnhf
references.authority: Long-running iterative agent loop and rollback behavior.
references.continuum_role: Emulate bounded journals, caps, rollback, and one-change discipline.
references.adoption_boundary: No live autonomous mutation before leases, budgets, adapters, and stop proof.
references.risk: Persistent loops can compound errors or collide with work.

references.name: treehouse
references.authority: Reusable isolated-worktree pool and cache-preserving mechanics.
references.continuum_role: Potential implementation behind Continuum worktree leases and topology.
references.adoption_boundary: Verify ownership, branch, HEAD, dirty state, and operations before reuse.
references.risk: Workspace reuse can leak stale state without leases.
references.next_experiment: Compare in-use detection with Continuum floor and lease requirements.

references.name: firstmate
references.continuum_role: Evaluate task shapes, isolation, and backend abstraction above Continuum.
references.adoption_boundary: Clone separately; crewmates receive Continuum packets and cannot override contracts.
references.risk: A second coordinator can split authority or multiply agents without controls.
references.next_experiment: Run one read-only scout against a disposable fixture.
